idea.title: Color Gate
idea.hook: Tap to bounce up through spinning color rings — slip through only the arc that matches your color.
idea.hook_ru: Нажимай, чтобы подпрыгивать вверх сквозь вращающиеся цветные кольца — проходи только через дугу своего цвета.
idea.prototype: Vertical PORTRAIT one-tap arcade-reflex climber. A glowing ball falls under constant gravity; the only input is a TAP anywhere = bounce up. Spinning obstacles ahead (single ring, counter-spinning double ring, sweeping line bar, rotating cross) are each split into bright colored arcs. The ball passes ONLY through the arc matching its current color; touching any other color = instant reset. The matching arc glows brighter than the rest so the goal is unmistakable in the first 5 seconds (the first obstacle is a trivially-easy single ring as an implicit tutorial). Paint-splat pickups swap the ball's color; star pickups accumulate toward cosmetic ball/trail unlocks. Difficulty escalates by stage (faster spin, more colors, denser obstacle mix) with milestone markers so progress is legible. Endless run with persistent best height + a daily-seeded run with its own best. Camera anchors the ball's peak so rings descend to meet it; stop tapping and the ball falls toward the bottom (losing ground) — non-timer pressure is gravity + wrong-color death, never a countdown.
idea.why_novel: Daily Dodge (our best-retaining arcade winner) and our other dodge/reflex games (parry_core, updrift) all ask the player to AVOID or ACT on incoming danger. Color Gate keeps the same proven arcade-reflex retention family but swaps the verb to a COLOR-MATCH read: a single ball, a single tap, and a perceptual decision — is the slot lined up in MY color? It folds Color-Switch's color-arc puzzle into a one-tap gravity climber, which neither Daily Dodge (lane-dodge) nor parry_core (4-edge parry) cover. The one-tap-anywhere control directly fixes Daily Dodge's only complaint ('mobile controls are not the best') and the recurring 'mobile vertical ui fucked / can't press continue' — every control is one-thumb-reachable in portrait.
idea.non_timer_pressure: constant gravity (the ball always falls; stop tapping and it drops off the bottom) + wrong-color death (spinning colored arcs you must time)
idea.lose_condition: gs='GAMEOVER' when the ball contacts a ring's bottom edge whose arc color != ball.color (one wrong-color contact), OR the ball falls off the bottom of the play field. No timer anywhere.

sources.name: portfolio knowledge 2026-05-27 (BUILD-MORE: Daily Dodge)
sources.note: KNOWLEDGE pick — inside-out from our own portfolio metrics. Daily Dodge (slug daily_dodge, genre dodge/arcade-reflex) is our single best-retaining game at 73.8s/play across 17 plays with 👍4/👎0 (100% likes) and is the lone BUILD-MORE winner this cycle. Color Gate extends that proven arcade-reflex retention family with a fresh color-match verb rather than re-shipping the dodge.

sources.name: arcade-reflex genre rollup + liked reflex games
sources.note: The dodge genre rollup shows 72.2s/play avg across 2 games at 👍5/👎0 (100%) — the highest-liked action family we have. Recent reflex builds parry_core ('100% good, develop more add to backlog to publish to web') and updrift ('overall interesting concept') were liked but flagged unclear-to-play; Color Gate bakes the 'crystal-clear goal in 5s' fix in as a hard requirement (glowing matching arc + trivial first gate + full EN/RU tutorial).

sources.name: player comment themes (unclear x13, mobile x9, needs-more-depth)
sources.note: Three recurring comment themes gated in as hard requirements: (1) 'unclear what to do' (x13: echo_locator, glacier_splitter, lucky_egg_bias, cargo_cult_vote, updrift, satisfying_spill…) -> matching arc glows, first obstacle trivially easy, 5-line EN+RU tutorial on MENU + '?' button; (2) 'mobile vertical ui fucked / can't press continue' (lucky_egg_bias, gerrymander, anti_ant_colony, shader_chip_loadout, wishlist_wheel) + Daily Dodge 'mobile controls not the best' -> one-tap-anywhere, all buttons large/thumb-reachable, designed+tested at 393x852 first; (3) 'needs more levels / meta / develop further' (satisfying_spill, tiny_markov_dunge, ice_sweeper) -> 4 obstacle types, escalating stages, star->cosmetic-unlock meta, daily seed, persistent best height.

sources.name: puzzle_objects CTR archetype
sources.note: CTR cover archetype puzzle_objects (our #1 archetype today): large tactile concentric COLOR RINGS with clearly separated color groups, glossy high-contrast, a glowing ball mid-near-pass about to slip through the matching arc. No title text (Yandex 5.6). Cover + icon generated via the CTR pipeline (gen_cover.sh, CF Flux).

synthesizer_brief: KNOWLEDGE-PICK (portfolio_knowledge origin). This extends our BUILD-MORE arcade winner Daily Dodge — our best-retaining game at 73.8s/play with 100% likes — rather than chasing a market signal. The bet: arcade-reflex is the proven-retention family for our audience (dodge genre 72.2s/play avg, 👍5/👎0), and parry_core + updrift confirmed reflex games get liked here. So we mine our own winner for a NEW one-tap reflex verb in that family. The novelty is a perceptual color-match read folded into a gravity climber: one ball, one tap-anywhere to bounce up, and the single decision 'is the slot lined up in MY color?' as spinning rings descend. Three hard requirements from our own player comments are baked in: (1) CRYSTAL-CLEAR GOAL IN 5s — the matching-color arc glows brighter than every other arc, the very first obstacle is a trivially-easy slow single ring with a huge matching arc, and the MENU + '?' panel carry a full 5-line EN+RU tutorial, so the mechanic teaches itself (kills the x13 'unclear what to do' complaint); (2) MOBILE-PORTRAIT-FIRST, ONE-THUMB — tap ANYWHERE on the 393x852 viewport bounces, and every PLAY/RETRY/DAILY/SKINS/? button is large and thumb-reachable in portrait, directly fixing Daily Dodge's 'mobile controls are not the best' and the recurring 'mobile vertical ui fucked / can't press continue'; (3) REAL DEPTH — 4 distinct obstacle types (single / counter-spinning double / sweeping line bar / rotating cross), per-stage escalation (faster spin, +colors, denser mix) with milestone markers, a star->cosmetic-unlock meta (5 ball skins), persistent best height, and a daily-seeded run with its own best, so there's a reason to replay. Cover uses the puzzle_objects archetype: glossy concentric multi-color rings, a glowing ball mid-near-pass, high contrast, no text.

alternatives.title: Splat Sweep
alternatives.note: A fresh cleaning-mechanic pick (cleaning is our most-LIKED genre at 👍15/👎1, 94%): a top-down ASMR paint/grime sweeper where you drag to clear themed rooms with layered breakable grime (the ice_sweeper request 'implement 2-3 layered breakable layers, add sounds, meta upgrades'). Rejected for THIS slot because the brief asked specifically for an arcade-reflex extension of Daily Dodge; held as a strong separate knowledge pick.

alternatives.title: Follow-Finger Survivor
alternatives.note: A dodge follow-finger survival: the ball tracks your finger/cursor continuously and you weave it through a bullet-hell of drifting hazards while a multiplier climbs — closer to Daily Dodge's literal 'move away from danger' fantasy. Rejected because it reuses the dodge verb verbatim (less novel) and continuous drag is exactly the 'not good for the laptop' / awkward-control complaint we keep getting; one-tap is more thumb-honest.

alternatives.title: Beat Gate
alternatives.note: A rhythm-reflex variant of Color Gate where rings lock to a musical beat and on-beat passes score a combo. A strong arcade-reflex direction but adds an audio/timing layer that risks the '5-second clear goal' mandate and needs sound assets (out of vanilla v0.1 scope). Held as a possible iteration-ladder step.

alternatives.title: Two-Tone Tunnel
alternatives.note: Strip to TWO colors and a continuous tunnel of alternating walls (classic Color-Switch minimalism) — easier to read but thinner on depth and very close to existing market clones. Rejected: the 4-color + 4-obstacle-type version directly answers the 'needs more depth' comment theme without losing first-5-seconds clarity.
